sample_tweets.text: [진짜 설문조사]이재명 신뢰 1.5% vs 尹 처벌 반대 99%… 베일 벗은 진짜 민심  미국 기반 여론조사 플랫폼 데모스폴 론칭 일주일 만에 해킹 공격… 복원된 데이터 속 충격적 수치 공개  한국 정치 여론조사에 대한 불신이 깊어지는 가운데, 조작 없는 100% 공정 여론조사를 표방하며 등장한 미국 기반 플랫폼 데모스폴(DemosPoll)이 출범 직후 해킹 공격을 받아 충격을 주고 있습니다.   특히 해킹 직전까지 집계된 여론조사 결과가 기존 국내 매체들의 보도와는 판이하게 다른 것으로 나타나 파장이 예상됩니다.  누가 이 수치를 두려워하나... 론칭 일주일 만의 기습 해킹  지난 4월 1일, 한국 내 외압과 규제로부터 자유로운 조사를 위해 미국 서버를 기반으로 베타 서비스를 시작한 데모스폴이 7일(미국 시간) 정체불명의 해커로부터 시스템 침입을 당했습니다. 운영진은 즉시 조사를 중단하고 데이터 복원에 나섰으며, 현재 약 90%의 데이터를 복구한 상태입니다.  전문가들은 이번 해킹에 대해 "누군가가 이 숫자가 세상에 나오는 것을 필사적으로 막고 싶어 하는 것 같다"며, 중국 측을 비롯한 국내외 여론 조작 조직의 개입 가능성을 제기했습니다.  복원된 데이터의 충격적 결과… 국내 여론조사와 정반대  해킹으로 중단되기 전까지 집계된 1,000명 목표의 테스트 데이터는 가히 파괴적입니다.   * 이재명 재판 관련: 응답자의 93.1%가 과거 재판은 절차대로 마무리되어야 한다고 답했습니다.   * 국정 운영 신뢰도: 이재명 정부의 국정운영 방향을 신뢰한다는 응답은 단 1.5%에 그쳤으며, 98.5%는 반대 의사를 분명히 했습니다.   * 대통령 처벌 여부: 윤석열 대통령 처벌에 대해서는 99.2%가 반대했으며, 찬성은 1명(0.8%)에 불과했습니다.   * 국회 해산: 22대 국회 조기 해산 및 재총선에 대해서는 97.7%가 압도적으로 찬성했습니다.  이러한 수치는 이재명 대표의 지지율이 70%에 육박한다는 기존 일부 여론조사 결과와 정면으로 배치되는 것이어서 민심의 날것이 드러난 것이 아니냐는 분석이 나오고 있습니다.   미국 서버 기반의 데이터 독립... 글로벌 리포트 발행 예고  데모스폴은 한국의 여론조사 공표 제한이나 정치적 압력에서 벗어나기 위해 미국 서버를 선택했습니다.  플랫폼 측은 1인 1표 원칙을 엄격히 적용하고 보트(Bot) 개입을 차단하는 기술적 장치를 마련했다고 밝혔습니다.  특히 이곳에서 수집된 데이터는 분석을 거쳐 영문과 국문으로 구성된 글로벌 리포트로 발행될 예정입니다.   이 보고서는 해외 주요 언론, 정부 기관, 싱크탱크 등에 배포되어 한국 사회의 실제 민심을 국제 사회에 전달하는 데이터 허브 역할을 할 것으로 기대됩니다.

한국과 청방 — 역사적 채무, 구조적 취약점, 그리고 현재 진행형  서론 — 당신이 지목한 핵심 역설 장제스와 쑹메이링이 한국 독립운동을 지원한 것은 역사적 사실입니다.  그러나 이것이 동시에 한국이 청방 네트워크에 구조적으로 채무 관계를 맺게 된 역사적 기원이기도 합니다.  이 역설을 풀어야 한국의 현재 상황을 정확히 이해할 수 있습니다.  (중략)  중국 측은 임시정부에 1억 위안과 20만 달러의 특별 보조금을 제공했습니다. 김구가 1937년 일본의 첩자에 의해 저격당해 위독했을 때, 장제스는 여러 차례 안부 전보를 보내고 측근을 파견해 거금의 치료비를 지원했습니다.   이것이 한국에서 장제스가 독립유공자로 지정된 역사적 배경입니다.  ✅그런데 두웨셩은 이 관계의 어디에 있는가? 청방의 핵심 수령 두웨셩과 장제스의 관계는 단순한 협력 관계가 아니었습니다.  청방은 1927년 장제스의 반공 쿠데타에서 핵심 역할을 했으며, 장제스는 두웨셩에게 국민혁명군 장군 계급을 부여했습니다.  청방은 아편 거래 수익을 국민당과 분배했으며, T.V. 쑹(쑹메이링의 오빠이자 국민당 재무장관)은 두웨셩과 협력해 상하이 은행들을 압박했습니다.   이것이 핵심입니다. 장제스가 임시정부에 제공한 자금의 일부는 청방(두웨셩)의 아편 무역 수익에서 나온 것이었습니다. 한국 독립운동은 의도하지 않았겠지만, 청방의 자금 네트워크와 연결된 자금을 받은 것입니다.  ✅장제스·쑹메이링의 "자유 대만" 프로파간다의 실체 당신이 정확히 지적한 부분입니다.  장제스와 쑹메이링은 대만 이전 후 "자유중국(Free China)"이라는 프레임을 구축했습니다.   이것의 목적은: 미국으로부터 지속적인 군사·경제 지원을 받기 위한 냉전 프레임 구축이었습니다.  한국전쟁 이후 자유민주주의 대 공산주의라는 이분법이 동아시아에 확산되면서, 대만은 이 프레임의 핵심 거점이 됩니다.  그리고 이 프레임 안에서 청방 네트워크는 "자유민주주의 수호자"의 외피를 얻습니다.  (중략)  ーーー  자료 분석 과정에서 나온 극히 일부의 결과인데, 왜 건드려선 안 될 것을 건드린 기분이 들까요?  이것도 조각을 다 맞추려면 시간이 꽤 걸릴 것 같습니다🥺  그들의 영향력은 과거의 일이 아니었습니다.  과거에는 아편이 그들의 무기였다면, 이제는 탈취한 기술이 그들의 무기입니다.  이는 여전히 현재진행형입니다.  거의 같은 시기에 여러 나라에서 동시다발적으로 발생한 기술 탈취 사건들은 소름 끼칠 정도로 동일한 프레임 안에서 일어났으며, 그 결과 그들은 세상의 정점에 서게 되었습니다.  이제는 더 깊고 강력한 영향력을 바탕으로, 세상은 그들의 설계대로 움직이고 있습니다.

IAEA는 SMR이 유연한 전력 공급, 산업열, 노후 화석연료 대체에 적합하다고 보고 있고, 여러 나라가 규제·인프라 준비를 확대 중입니다. 다만 이 업계는 기대감은 크지만 상용화 속도는 느린 편이라, 단기 모멘텀보다 중장기 관찰용에 가깝습니다.  체크할 기술 SMR 설계/제어, 원전 기자재, 연료 공급망,  원전용 디지털 계측제어  7) 배터리: EV보다 에너지저장장치(ESS) 쪽 비중 확대 배터리는 여전히 중요하지만, 예전처럼 “전기차만 보면 된다”는 구간은 아닙니다. IEA는 EV 배터리 수요가 2024년 약 1TWh에서 2030년 3TWh 이상으로 늘 것으로 봤습니다. 다만 최근은 EV 경쟁 심화와 가격 압박이 있는 반면, 전력망 불안과 재생에너지 확대 때문에 ESS와 전력 저장의 중요성이 더 커지고 있습니다.  체크할 기술 LFP 중심 원가 경쟁, ESS용 배터리, 배터리 재활용 BMS, 고체전해질은 장기 관찰  8) GLP-1 비만치료제·생산설비·관련 헬스케어 의약 쪽에서는 GLP-1 계열이 여전히 큰 축입니다. FDA는 세마글루타이드 주사제 부족이 2025년 2월 해소됐다고 밝혔고, 공급 정상화 이후에는 제조능력, 보험 적용, 복용 편의성(경구제) 쪽이 다음 경쟁 포인트가 되고 있습니다. 즉, 완제품 제약사뿐 아니라 CDMO, 주사제 생산, 펜 디바이스, 원료 공급도 같이 볼 필요가 있습니다.  체크할 기술 경구 GLP-1, 장기 지속형 제형, 주사제 생산설비 비만 데이터, 플랫폼/합병증 관리

"Turn your spare WiFi into passive income."  M&A risk assessment of "Bandwidth Sharing": You’re selling your network security and IP reputation for pennies.   The math: If your bandwidth generates $5/mo but your network is used for a DDoS attack or illicit activity, the legal cost is a 10,000x multiple of your earnings.   In corporate IT, we call this an "unauthorized exit point." In legal terms, it’s a liability magnet.   Due diligence: You're not "powering decentralized AI." You're providing a low-cost, unmonitored entry point for bad actors.   The yield doesn't justify the catastrophic tail risk. 🤡

'원사이즈(One-size-fits-all)'의 함정을 파고들어라  존 맥닐은 부의 기회가 어디에 있는지 정확히 짚어줍니다. 바로 모두에게 똑같이 적용되는 서비스가 제공되는 곳입니다.  불균형의 발견: 보안 위험이 전혀 다른 두 기업이 똑같은 금액의 사이버 보험료를 내고 있다면? 거기엔 반드시 비효율이 있고, 그 비효율이 곧 사업 기회입니다.  시장 세분화: 클라우드용 보안 툴은 수백 개지만, 중소기업(SMB)을 위한 보안 툴은 전무했습니다. 남들이 보지 않는 '불편한 영역'을 공략하는 것이 유니콘으로 가는 지름길입니다.  핵심 질문: "왜 이 마찰이 존재하는가?", "어떻게 하면 이 과정을 0으로 만들 수 있는가?"를 끊임없이 질문하십시오. . . . 앤트로픽의 새 AI 모델 미토스로 인한 보안 위험에 대해 미 연준 의장과 재무장관이 은행 CEO들에게 경고  미 재무장관과 연방준비제도 의장은 지난 화요일 주요 은행 CEO들을 긴급 소집해 앤트로픽의 미토스와 관련된 사이버 보안 위험에 대해 경고.  재무부 청사에서 열린 회의에서 금융 기관들이 미토스 및 유사 모델이 공격 노출 위험에 어떤 영향을 미칠 수 있는지 이해하고 적절한 방어 체계를 구축하도록 촉구.  앤트로픽은 앞서 미토스 공개하면서 이 모델이 이전에 확인되지 않았던 사이버 보안 취약점을 드러낼 수 있다며 일반에는 공개하지 않기로 결정. 미토스가 현재 널리 사용되는 모든 주요 운영체제와 브라우저의 보안 허점을 찾아내고 이를 악용할 수 있다고 설명.  앤트로픽은 프로젝트 글래스윙 통해 미토스 접근 권한을 12개 출범 파트너사로 제한하면서 방어적 보안 작업을 위해 최대 1억 달러 상당의 사용 크레딧을 제공하기로 약속.  중요 소프트웨어 인프라를 구축하거나 유지 관리하는 40개 이상의 추가 기관에도 접근 권한 부여. 로이터는 마이크로소프트와 구글을 포함한 약 40개 기술 기업으로 제한했다고 보도한 반면, 블룸버그는 아마존과 애플도 포함됐다고 보도.  앤트로픽은 미토스 공개 전 연방 당국자들과의 이 모델이 디지털 시스템을 공격하고 방어하는 데 어떤 역할을 할 수 있는지에 대해 논의했음을 시인. qz.com/bessent-powell-bank-c…

You just extracted a suspicious URL from a phishing email. Do you click it? Spin up a sandbox? Or validate it first?  The DFIR Suite API's POST /phishing/safe-browsing endpoint checks URLs against Google Safe Browsing — a threat intelligence database covering billions of URLs with real-time updates. It flags URLs associated with phishing, malware, unwanted software, and social engineering.  Practical scenario: You're triaging a spearphishing campaign targeting your organization. Multiple emails contain shortened or obfuscated links. You extract the URLs, run them through the API, and immediately identify which ones are flagged by Google. No sandbox required. No accidental clicks. Just fast, reliable threat validation during initial triage.  Key features: • Checks against Google's continuously updated threat lists • Returns threat type (MALWARE, SOCIAL_ENGINEERING, UNWANTED_SOFTWARE, etc.) and platform information • Costs 2 credits per request • Available on all DFIR Suite plans  This is the kind of tool that reduces risk and accelerates phishing investigations. If you're handling email-based threats, this endpoint should be part of your workflow.  #DFIR #DFIRSuite #ThreatIntel #PhishingAnalysis #CyberSecurity

🌍 Where is your adversary actually operating from?  Every phishing email, every malicious domain, every piece of threat infrastructure has a physical footprint. IP addresses don't lie about geography — even when threat actors do.  DFIR Suite's POST /phishing/geoip endpoint gives you precise geolocation data for any IP address: country, city, region, ISP, and ASN. One credit per lookup. Built for analysts who process dozens or hundreds of IOCs per investigation.  Real-world use case: You're analyzing a business email compromise attempt. The attacker claims to be your CFO, emailing urgently from a London conference. But email header analysis reveals the originating IP geolocates to Lagos, Nigeria. Impossible travel. Red flag confirmed. Incident contained before wire transfer goes out.  Another scenario: You're tracking a phishing kit across multiple campaigns. Five domains, all active, all resolving to different IPs. Run them through /phishing/geoip. Four IPs cluster in the same ASN in Romania. One outlier in Singapore. Now you understand the infrastructure topology: shared hosting vs. backup nodes. You know where to focus takedown coordination.  Key features: • Returns country, city, region, ISP, and ASN for any IP • 1 credit per request — affordable for bulk IOC enrichment • Combine with /phishing/blacklist for full IP profiling (location + reputation) • Map threat actor infrastructure across investigations • Detect geographic anomalies and impossible travel scenarios  Geolocation isn't the flashiest capability in your toolkit. But it's foundational. When you need to know where the adversary is operating from — and whether their story holds up — this is the call you make.  API base: hXXps://api[.]dfir-lab[.]ch/v1 Endpoint: POST /phishing/geoip  #DFIR #DFIRSuite #ThreatIntel #PhishingAnalysis #CyberSecurity

Eric Zimmerman's Tools: The Windows DFIR parsing suite you can't work without.  If you're doing digital forensics or incident response on Windows systems, Eric Zimmerman's collection of free CLI tools is non-negotiable. This suite has become the industry standard for parsing critical Windows artifacts — and for good reason.  Key tools include: • MFTECmd — parses Master File Table entries at lightning speed (millions of records in seconds) • PECmd — Prefetch file analysis for program execution artifacts • LECmd — LNK file parsing for user activity reconstruction • JLECmd — Jump list analysis • RECmd — Registry hive parsing with custom plugins • EvtxECmd — Windows Event Log parsing, faster than most commercial alternatives • SrumECmd — System Resource Usage Monitor database analysis • AmcacheParser — application execution and shimcache artifact extraction  What makes these tools exceptional is their speed and the ecosystem around them. Timeline Explorer, the companion GUI, lets you load and filter the CSV/TSV output from any EZ tool, making it easy to correlate artifacts across multiple sources during an investigation.  These tools are core curriculum in SANS FOR508 and FOR500 courses, and they're deployed in IR teams worldwide. They're actively maintained, well-documented, and built by a practitioner who understands real-world forensic workflows.  Available at ericzimmerman[.]github.io #DFIRTools #DigitalForensics #IncidentResponse #OpenSource #InfoSec

The Recycle Bin isn't just a safety net for users — it's a goldmine for DFIR practitioners.  When a file is deleted through Explorer, Windows creates two artifacts in C:\﹩Recycle[.]Bin\<User SID>\:  • ﹩I files store metadata: original file path, deletion timestamp (UTC), and file size • ﹩R files contain the actual deleted file content, recoverable until the bin is emptied  Each user account gets a separate folder identified by SID, letting you attribute deletions to specific users — critical when investigating insider threats or data exfiltration attempts.  Parse with RBCmd (Zimmerman), rifiuti2, or Autopsy. In IR scenarios, checking the Recycle Bin can reveal what an attacker tried to hide: deleted logs, staging directories, or tools they cleaned up post-compromise. The ﹩I metadata survives even if the ﹩R content is overwritten, preserving the deletion timeline.  MAPPED TO MITRE: T1070.004 (Indicator Removal: File Deletion)  #DFIR #DigitalForensics

US intelligence assessments over the last 20 years (roughly 2005–2025) have consistently indicated that Iran is not an imminent threat to the United States in terms of a nuclear weapon or direct existential attack capability.   This holds particularly for nuclear weaponization and delivery systems capable of reaching the US homeland.  Key points from major reports include:  •  2007 National Intelligence Estimate (NIE) on Iran’s Nuclear Intentions and Capabilities:   While Iran continued uranium enrichment (which could support future weaponization if decided), the halt reduced the perceived urgency of an imminent nuclear threat.  Source: Key Judgments  (unclassified summary)  iranwatch.org/sites/default/…  This 2007 finding has been reiterated or aligned with in subsequent assessments, emphasizing no active weaponization decision by Iran’s leadership (particularly Supreme Leader Khamenei).  •  Annual Threat Assessments (ATA) and related reports (2010s–2025): These yearly unclassified summaries from the Office of the Director of National Intelligence (ODNI) and the broader Intelligence Community repeatedly stated that Iran was not building a nuclear weapon and had not reauthorized the weapons program suspended in 2003. For example:  •  In the 2025 ATA (released March 2025): “We continue to assess Iran is not building a nuclear weapon and that Khamenei has not reauthorized the nuclear weapons program he suspended in 2003.” It noted erosion of taboos on discussing nuclear weapons publicly and high enriched uranium stockpiles (unprecedented for a non-nuclear state), but no active weapon-building. Iran posed regional threats via missiles, proxies, and cyber, but not an imminent homeland nuclear threat. Source: 2025 Annual Threat Assessment (PDF) dni.gov/files/ODNI/documents…  •  Similar language appeared in prior years (e.g., 2022–2024 ATAs), with Iran described as advancing capabilities that could shorten breakout time (time to produce fissile material for a bomb) but not pursuing weaponization. Breakout estimates focused on material production (months to weeks in later years), not full weapon delivery.  •  Delivery Systems and Homeland Threat: US intelligence has long assessed Iran lacks intercontinental ballistic missiles (ICBMs) capable of reaching the US.   A 2025 Defense Intelligence Agency report concluded Iran had no such missiles and might need until 2035 or longer for even a limited capability, requiring a major push. Earlier predictions (e.g., 1999 NIE forecasting ICBM threat by 2015) did not materialize.   Iran’s missiles and drones threaten the region (e.g., US forces, allies like Israel), but not the US homeland imminently.  Source: Arms Control Association analysis referencing DIA 2025 report  armscontrol.org/issue-briefs…

sample_tweets.text: NADA report data is out and the opportunity most dealers are missing is used cars.  The latest NADA data shows franchised dealers sold 13.1 million used cars in 2025 (Source: NADA)  Sounds strong… until you look deeper.  Independent dealers moved 9.8 million used cars in the same year (Source: NIADA)  That’s ~75% of franchise dealer used volume happening completely outside the franchise network.  Now layer this in: •CarMax: ~790,000 units •Carvana: ~596,000 units  That’s ~1.4 million used cars from just TWO companies (Source: Company Reports)  They didn’t chase new cars. They built their entire model around used.  Here’s where it gets real for dealers:  The average store is doing: •~79 new cars/month •~45–50 used cars/month (based on NADA mix)  But the market is telling you something different…  There’s easily 20–40 more used units per month sitting on the table in most stores.  Not because of demand. Because of execution.  The gap isn’t traffic.  It’s: •Inventory acquisition •Speed to market •Pricing discipline •Follow-up consistency •Merchandising  Independent dealers and Carvana didn’t win because they had better locations.  They won because they built a used-first machine.  Meanwhile, most franchise stores still treat used like a side department.  The opportunity is simple:  Stop thinking: “We sell used cars too.”  Start thinking: “We dominate used… and new is a bonus.”  The stores that figure that out are going to take share. Fast.

Anthropic chegou a US$ 30 bilhões de receita anual sendo a empresa que mais disse não.  Sem multimodal. Sem vídeo. Sem hardware. Sem chips. Sem data center próprio.  Enquanto a OpenAI expandia em todas as direções, a Anthropic escolheu uma aposta: coding e co-work. 2.500 pessoas puxando na mesma direção.  O resultado foi o ramp de receita mais rápido da história da tecnologia.  Tenho usado esse caso com clientes que me perguntam sobre concentração de portfolio.  Concentração por convicção é diferente de concentração por descuido.   A Anthropic não ficou focada porque não tinha opção. Ficou focada porque fez uma aposta explícita: se AGI vai existir, vai ser construída escrevendo código.  A tese pode estar errada. Mas o resultado por enquanto é inegável.  Quando analiso um portfolio muito concentrado, a primeira pergunta que faço não é "quanto você está em risco". A pergunta é: "qual é a sua tese, e o que precisaria ser verdade para ela estar errada?"  Se a pessoa não sabe responder, o problema não é a concentração.

sample_tweets.text: A Lone Wolf Russian Hacker is stepping into the spotlight.  We’ve just completed an exclusive interview with PalachPro, an individual actor who claims no affiliation with any group and operates independently. His name has surfaced across multiple intelligence reports, regional media, and cyber conflict narratives tied to ongoing geopolitical tensions.  Before we publish the full interview on Daily Dark Web, we want to open the floor to our community.  If you had the chance to ask a self-claimed independent Russian hacker anything, what would it be?  Drop your questions below. The most relevant and challenging ones will be included in the final release.

🇮🇹 Alleged Data Sale: Italian Citizens Database (353K Records)  A threat actor is offering a database containing personal data of Italian individuals, including highly sensitive national identifiers.  📂 Key Details: • 353,742 records • Price model: €0.02 per record (~€7K full dataset estimate) • Payment: Monero (XMR) • Sold in bulk or segmented exports  🧠 Claimed Data Includes: • Full names • Physical addresses • Phone numbers • Codice Fiscale (Italian Tax ID)  🧠 Assessment: • Presence of Codice Fiscale significantly increases sensitivity (national identifier) • Pricing model aligns with structured, monetizable datasets rather than low-effort dumps • “Completely new” claim → unverified, but common sales tactic • Dataset size suggests: •Niche source (regional, sector-specific), or •Aggregated dataset rather than a single breach  ⚠️ Key Risks: • Identity theft and impersonation within Italy • Targeted phishing and social engineering • Financial fraud leveraging tax ID + contact details • Potential GDPR exposure and regulatory consequences  🔍 What to Watch: • Sample validation or reuse in other forums • Correlation with known Italian breaches • Increased phishing targeting Italian citizens • Law enforcement or CERT-IT alerts  ⚠️ Status: Unverified – High sensitivity due to national ID exposure  #DataBreach #Italy #PII #ThreatIntel #DarkWeb

🚨 Credential Tool Circulating: “Hunter Mix Inboxer” (Cracked Version)  A threat actor is sharing a cracked version of a combo checker / inbox validator tool, commonly used to process large credential dumps (URL:LOGIN:PASS).  🧠 What This Tool Does: • Validates email:password combinations at scale • Checks for active inbox access (IMAP/SMTP) • Filters valid accounts for further abuse • Uses keyword-based parsing (e.g., PayPal, Coinbase, Instagram) • Supports proxy usage and threading for automation  🧠 Why This Matters: • Tools like this are critical in the credential abuse ecosystem • Enable attackers to turn raw combo lists into high-value, verified accounts • Often used in: •Account takeover (ATO) campaigns •Financial fraud (crypto, banking, e-commerce) •Phishing follow-ups using real inbox access  🧠 Notable Signals: • “Cracked” version → lower barrier to entry for less-skilled actors • Integrated keyword filtering → targeted monetization (crypto, fintech, social) • Telegram distribution → typical rapid spread channel  ⚠️ Key Risks: • Increased success rate of credential stuffing attacks • Rapid scaling of ATO operations • Abuse of compromised inboxes for internal phishing / BEC  🔍 Defensive Considerations: • Enforce MFA across all external-facing services • Monitor for suspicious IMAP/SMTP logins • Detect impossible travel / anomalous access patterns • Encourage password hygiene and credential rotation  ⚠️ Status: Active tool circulation – supports ongoing credential-based attacks  #CyberThreat #CredentialStuffing #ATO #ThreatIntel #DarkWeb

Alleged Leak: Spanish City Hall Email Dump (150K+ Emails)  A threat actor is offering a full email dump from a mid-tier Spanish municipality, including attachments and internal communications.  📂 Key Details: • 150,000+ emails • Includes attachments + full mailbox data • Price: $8,000 (XMR) • Access via brokered / guarantor-based deal  🧠 Claimed Scope: • ~43 corporate mailboxes • Covers entire city hall operations • Includes public entities linked to municipality • Population context: ~28,000 residents  🧠 Likely Data Exposure: • Internal government communications • Citizen data exchanged via email • Contracts, invoices, legal documents • Credentials or sensitive attachments • Operational and administrative discussions  🧠 Assessment: • More credible pricing model compared to typical scams • Structured “inventory-style” description → suggests organized exfiltration • Could indicate: •Email server compromise (e.g., OWA / Exchange) •Credential harvesting + mailbox scraping • High probability of phishing, BEC, and follow-on attacks  ⚠️ Key Risks: • Exposure of citizen personal data • Business Email Compromise (BEC) campaigns using real threads • Abuse of government authority context for fraud • Potential GDPR implications and regulatory impact  🔍 What to Watch: • Emergence of samples in other forums • Mentions of specific municipality name • CERT / Spanish authority notifications • Secondary sales or data leaks  ⚠️ Status: Unverified – Moderate to High credibility signal  #DataBreach #Spain #CyberThreat #ThreatIntel #DarkWeb

🇫🇷 Alleged Data Sale: France Travail (10M+ Records)  A threat actor is advertising a database claimed to belong to France Travail (formerly Pôle emploi), France’s national employment service.  📂 Key Details: • 10+ million records • Price: $350 (notably low for claimed scale) • Data presented as “fresh” and “private”  🧠 Claimed Data Exposure: • NIR (French national identification number) • First & last names • Account / employee identifiers • Agency (Pôle emploi office) • ZIP code • Timestamps • Internal employment-related references (RCI, agency codes)  🧠 Assessment: • If valid, this is highly sensitive PII exposure: •NIR = critical identity number (similar to SSN) • However, pricing is suspiciously low → possible: •Resold / partial dataset •Old breach repackaged •Scam or low-quality sample • Still aligns with common public-sector targeting patterns  ⚠️ Key Risks: • Identity theft and fraud targeting French citizens • Phishing campaigns impersonating government services • Abuse of employment-related systems and benefits  🔍 What to Watch: • Validation via leaked samples or external confirmation • Appearance of dataset in multiple forums • Alerts from French authorities (CNIL, CERT-FR)  ⚠️ Status: Unverified claim – high sensitivity, questionable pricing  #DataBreach #France #PII #CyberThreat #ThreatIntel

🚨 Massive Credential Leak Advertised: 448M+ Records (URL:LOG:PASS)  A threat actor is offering a large credential dataset commonly associated with infostealer infections and combo lists.  📂 Key Details: • 448+ million credentials • Size: ~25.1 GB • Format: URL : Username : Password • Claim: Duplicates removed (unique lines only)  🧠 What This Indicates: • Typical aggregation of: •Infostealer logs (RedLine, Raccoon, Vidar, etc.) •Previous breaches and credential stuffing lists • “Unique lines” suggests: •Cleaned dataset optimized for credential stuffing attacks  ⚠️ Key Risks: • Large-scale account takeover (ATO) campaigns • Automated attacks against: •Email providers •Banking / fintech •SaaS and enterprise platforms • Increased success rate due to: •Credential reuse across services  🛑 Critical Insight: • This is not necessarily a new breach • It is a weaponized aggregation, making it more dangerous operationally  🛡️ Defensive Actions: • Enforce MFA across all accounts • Monitor for credential stuffing patterns (high-volume login attempts) • Implement IP reputation + rate limiting • Encourage users to avoid password reuse  🔍 What to Watch: • Distribution across Telegram / dark web markets • Spikes in login failures or suspicious access attempts • Targeted campaigns using subsets of this dataset  ⚠️ Status: High credibility – common but highly impactful threat activity  #DataLeak #CredentialStuffing #CyberThreat #Infostealer #ThreatIntel

Alleged Database Leak: Korean Academic Association (KAAC)  A threat actor claims to have leaked a database linked to kaac.dothome.co.kr, associated with a Korean academic organization using the XpressEngine (XE) CMS.  📂 Key Details: • Size: ~830MB SQL database • Records: 600,000+ entries • Region: South Korea • Includes admin logs and system-level data  🧠 Claimed Data Exposure: • Admin logs with IP addresses • Session logs • System configuration data • Member registration data • Document / board content • File attachment metadata  🧠 Assessment: • Presence of admin + session logs significantly increases risk: •Potential for session hijacking or replay analysis •Mapping of administrator activity and infrastructure • XE CMS has historically had security weaknesses when not properly patched/configured • Likely compromise vectors: •Web application vulnerability •Weak admin credentials •Misconfigured hosting environment  ⚠️ Key Risks: • Account takeover of admins or members • Exposure of internal communications / academic content • Follow-on attacks using infrastructure intelligence • Targeting of Korean academic/research ecosystem  🔍 What to Watch: • Validation via sample data or independent confirmation • Additional leaks from same infrastructure • Exploitation of exposed sessions or credentials  ⚠️ Status: Unverified claim – technically plausible, moderate to high risk  #DataBreach #CyberThreat #SouthKorea #Academia #ThreatIntel

🇲🇦 🚨 Alleged Data Leak: Morocco OFPPT (Office de la Formation Professionnelle)  A threat actor is claiming to have access to a database linked to OFPPT, Morocco’s national vocational training organization.  📂 Key Details: • Target: OFPPT (Office de la Formation Professionnelle et de la Promotion du Travail) • Data advertised on underground forum by a VIP-level user • Likely includes institutional or student-related records (based on target profile)  🧠 Assessment: • OFPPT is a high-value public sector entity with: •Large student population •Workforce and training data • Potential breach vectors: •Web application vulnerabilities •Compromised credentials •Third-party or contractor access  ⚠️ Potential Data Exposure: • Personal information (students, staff) • Administrative or internal records • Contact details, IDs, or enrollment data  ⚠️ Key Risks: • Identity theft targeting students and employees • Phishing campaigns using institutional context • Reputational and regulatory impact for public sector  🔍 What to Watch: • Sample data release or proof-of-breach • Official response from Moroccan authorities • Spread of dataset to other forums  ⚠️ Status: Unverified claim – moderate to high risk if confirmed  #DataBreach #CyberThreat #Morocco #PublicSector #ThreatIntel

🇪🇬 Multiple Egyptian Government Websites Reported Down (Hacktivist Claims)  The hacktivist group Keymous Plus is claiming disruption of several Egyptian government websites, suggesting a coordinated campaign.  📢 Reported Targets: • Egyptian State Information Service (sis.gov.eg) • Ministry of Petroleum (petroleum.gov.eg) • Ministry of Trade & Industry (mti.gov.eg) • Ministry of Justice (jp.gov.eg) • Ministry of Finance (mof.gov.eg) • Egypt Government Portal (egypt.gov.eg)  🧠 Assessment: • Activity pattern strongly aligns with DDoS or service disruption campaigns • Use of “site down” checks suggests: •Focus on availability disruption, not necessarily breach • Messaging and timing indicate: •Coordinated hacktivist operation •Likely part of broader geopolitical or ideological campaign  ⚠️ Key Considerations: • “Down” status ≠ confirmed compromise • Could be: •Temporary outages •Traffic flooding (DDoS) •Or even false positives from monitoring tools • No evidence (yet) of: •Data exfiltration •Persistent access  🔍 What to Watch: • Official confirmation from Egyptian authorities • Duration of outages (minutes vs sustained disruption) • Escalation into defacement or data leaks  ⚠️ Status: Unverified disruption claims – likely DDoS activity  #CyberThreat #Hacktivism #Egypt #DDoS #ThreatIntel

🚨 Handala Claims Ongoing Cyberattacks on UAE Critical Infrastructure  The threat actor Handala is claiming an active cyber campaign targeting critical infrastructure in the UAE, with significant data destruction.  📢 Key Claims: • Ongoing coordinated cyberattacks • Target: UAE critical infrastructure • Alleged impact: 6+ petabytes of data destroyed • Data reportedly wiped or deleted (destructive attack) • More “evidence” promised imminently  🧠 Assessment: • The messaging strongly indicates a wiper-style operation, not traditional ransomware • The scale (petabytes destroyed) is likely exaggerated — common in hacktivist/propaganda communications • However, intent signals: •Disruption-focused operations •Psychological impact and narrative control • Group behavior aligns with information operations + cyber disruption hybrid activity  ⚠️ Key Risks: • Potential real disruptions to energy, telecom, or logistics sectors • Follow-on attacks or opportunistic actors leveraging the situation • Panic amplification via unverified large-scale destruction claims  🔍 What to Watch: • Official statements from UAE authorities or affected entities • Technical indicators (wiper malware, outages, service degradation) • Release of “proof” (often partial or recycled data)  ⚠️ Status: Unverified claim – high likelihood of exaggeration, but operational intent credible  #CyberThreat #Hacktivism #CriticalInfrastructure #UAE #ThreatIntel

🚨 Fresh RedLine Stealer Logs Advertised for Sale  A threat actor is offering a large dataset of infostealer logs, allegedly collected via RedLine malware, a widely used credential-stealing tool.  📂 Claimed Dataset: • 6,758 fresh logs (dated April 2026) • “Unwrapped” → likely raw, ready-to-use data • Distributed via private channels / gated access  🧠 What This Means: • RedLine logs typically contain: •Browser-stored credentials (logins/passwords) •Cookies & active session tokens •Autofill data, crypto wallets, system info • These logs are often used for: •Account takeovers (ATO) •Initial access for ransomware / intrusion •Bypassing MFA using session hijacking  ⚠️ Key Risks: • Immediate exploitation of exposed accounts • Corporate credential reuse → enterprise compromise • Access to SaaS platforms (VPN, email, cloud apps)  🛡️ Defensive Actions: • Force password resets for exposed users • Invalidate sessions and cookies (critical) • Monitor for unusual logins / impossible travel • Deploy endpoint detection for infostealer activity • Educate users on malware delivery vectors (cracks, phishing, loaders)  🔍 What to Watch: • Correlation with known stealer campaigns • Reuse of logs across multiple forums • Targeting of specific industries or regions  ⚠️ Status: Ongoing infostealer activity – highly credible threat pattern  #Infostealer #RedLine #CyberThreat #CredentialLeak #ThreatIntel

📑Alleged Adobe Reader 0-Day / 1-Day PDF Exploit Advertised  A threat actor is claiming to sell an exploit targeting Adobe Acrobat / Reader, leveraging a vulnerability in the JavaScript engine.  📂 Key Claims: • Zero-day / one-day exploit in Adobe Reader • Abuses privileged Acrobat JavaScript APIs • Enables remote code execution (RCE) • Delivered via a malicious PDF file (appears legitimate) • Designed for email-based delivery (phishing attachment)  🧠 Assessment: • If valid, this is high-impact due to: •Ubiquity of PDF usage across enterprises •Trusted file format → high phishing success rate • Likely scenarios: •True 0-day (rare but critical) •Repackaged or obfuscated known vulnerability (1-day exploit) •Scam or low-quality exploit (common in forums)  ⚠️ Key Risks: • Initial access vector for APT or ransomware campaigns • Bypass of traditional detection via legitimate-looking PDFs • Potential targeting of enterprise users, finance, legal teams  🛡️ Defensive Considerations: • Disable or restrict JavaScript execution in PDF readers • Enforce email attachment sandboxing • Monitor for suspicious PDF behavior (child processes, network calls) • Ensure Adobe products are fully patched and updated  🔍 What to Watch: • CVE disclosures or Adobe security advisories • Similar exploit chatter across multiple forums • Campaigns leveraging weaponized PDFs  ⚠️ Status: Unverified claim – high risk if confirmed  #CyberSecurity #ZeroDay #Adobe #Malware #ThreatIntel

🐊 Alleged Supply Chain Data Sale Targeting Global Retail Brands  A threat actor is claiming access to supply chain data associated with multiple well-known fashion and apparel brands.  🏢 Targeted Brands: • Lacoste • Ralph Lauren • Canada Goose • Carters • New Era • Spanx • And others  📂 Claimed Access: • “Full supply chain data” (likely includes vendors, manufacturing, logistics, or internal documents) • Mentions of FTP access, shells, SQL injection, and server/database access • Actor claims exclusivity: “not sold or leaked before”  💰 Sale Details: • Starting price: $50,000+ • Escrow accepted (suggests marketplace-based transaction)  🧠 Assessment: • This is not a typical customer data leak — it’s potentially more critical: •Exposure of supplier networks and manufacturing pipelines •Insight into pricing, sourcing, and operational dependencies • Possible origins: •Compromise of third-party vendors •Exploitation via SQL injection or weak infrastructure access controls  ⚠️ Key Risks: • Supply chain attacks (pivoting into trusted partners) • Counterfeit production using real supplier data • Competitive intelligence abuse • Targeted ransomware against suppliers  🔍 What to Watch: • Any confirmation from affected brands • Indicators of data reuse in other forums • Signs of downstream attacks targeting suppliers  ⚠️ Status: Unverified claim – high strategic impact if validated  #DataBreach #SupplyChain #CyberThreat #RetailSecurity #ThreatIntel

🇷🇸 Alleged Data Sale: Football Association of Serbia (FSS) Database  A threat actor claims to be selling a database linked to the Football Association of Serbia (fss.rs), containing player-related records from multiple countries.  📂 Claimed Dataset: • ~560,000 total records • ~47,000 unique players worldwide • Includes both Serbian citizens and international players  📊 Alleged Data Exposure: • First & last names • Country of birth • National identifiers (JMBG for Serbian citizens) • Passport / Visa IDs / SSNs (for foreign players)  🧠 Assessment: • High-risk dataset due to presence of government-issued identifiers • Likely scenarios: •Compromise of sports federation systems or third-party data processors •Or aggregation of multiple sports-related datasets • Actor claims attempted disclosure to the organization → suggests extortion-style pressure  ⚠️ Key Risks: • Identity theft involving professional athletes and staff • Targeted fraud or impersonation using official IDs • Potential reputational and regulatory impact on the organization  🔍 What to Watch: • Confirmation from FSS or national authorities • Leak distribution across other forums or Telegram • Use of data in fraud or betting-related abuse  ⚠️ Status: Unverified claim – high sensitivity due to government ID exposure  #DataBreach #CyberThreat #SportsSecurity #IdentityTheft #ThreatIntel

🇻🇳 Alleged Data Breach: Vietnam Flight Booking System (Passport Data)  A threat actor is advertising a large dataset allegedly sourced from a flight booking system in Vietnam, containing passport data and traveler information from global passengers.  📂 Claimed Dataset: • ~1.1M+ records with passport data • Includes full passport images + contact details • Global coverage (US, UK, EU, Asia, Middle East, etc.) • Ongoing access claimed (“can provide daily passports”)  💰 Sale Details: • ~$0.10 per passport record • Country-specific targeting available • Sample images and contact data shared publicly  🧠 Assessment: • High-risk travel sector data exposure involving: •Identity documents (passports) •Personally identifiable information (PII) • Extremely low pricing suggests: •Either mass-scale dataset •Or recycled/previously leaked data • Claim of ongoing access indicates potential active compromise or insider access  ⚠️ Key Risks: • Identity theft using passport-grade data • Fraud across: •Travel platforms •Financial services (KYC bypass) • Targeting of international travelers  🔍 What to Watch: • Validation of passport authenticity • Overlap with known airline/travel breaches • Emergence of fraud campaigns using this data  ⚠️ Status: Unverified claim – critical sensitivity due to passport exposure  #DataBreach #TravelSecurity #CyberThreat #IdentityTheft #ThreatIntel

🇮🇩 Alleged Data Leak: Indonesian Social Assistance System (SAPA Bansos)  A threat actor claims to have leaked a database tied to Indonesia’s SAPA Bansos (social aid) system, exposing highly sensitive citizen information.  📂 Claimed Dataset: • ~1.2 million records • Government-related platform (sapabansos.jatim.go.id) • Focus: social assistance beneficiaries  📊 Alleged Data Exposure: • National ID numbers (NIK) • Full names (including family relations) • Addresses (district, sub-district, village) • Date of birth & gender • Bank account details (Bank Jatim) • Social aid eligibility/status  🧠 Assessment: • Likely government database exposure or aggregation of welfare records • Data structure suggests official administrative dataset, not random scraping • If valid, this represents: •Mass PII exposure of vulnerable populations •Potential insider leak or misconfigured system  ⚠️ Key Risks: • Identity theft and financial fraud • Targeting of low-income / vulnerable individuals • Abuse of banking details for account fraud or scams • Social engineering leveraging government program data  🔍 What to Watch: • Confirmation from Indonesian authorities • Overlap with previous government data leaks • Emergence of fraud campaigns targeting beneficiaries  ⚠️ Status: Unverified claim – high impact due to government + financial data exposure  #DataBreach #CyberThreat #Government #Indonesia #ThreatIntel

If the Automated Clearing House (ACH) goes down, the money stops moving entirely.  That is exactly what happened when the Conquerors Electronic Army (CEA) set their sights on MASAV, the invisible backbone of Israel’s financial system.  We aren't talking about a simple website defacement. We are talking about a sophisticated strike against the "single point of failure" that processes:  Every payroll deposit. Every government pension. Every utility direct debit. Every interbank transfer in the nation.  The shift is chilling: Hacktivists are moving away from "loud" symbolic targets and moving toward the quiet, critical infrastructure that actually keeps a country running. Time to nerd schizo rant for a second.  "Hxr1" writes you can abuse the newly minted Windows Machine Learning API to execute shellcode in-memory without having to invoke VirtualAlloc.  That is wrong ... because he himself in his proof-of-concept invokes VirtualAlloc, so I don't know why he wrote that.  However, this paper is an interesting concept on smuggling malicious code and/or payloads. Without going on a deeper schizo rant however, I'd really like to emphasize this proof-of-concept does indeed work, but it has bugs... but, whatever.  Basically Windows Runtime has a thingie that allows you to run pretty shrimple AI models (ONNX). It's called the Windows Machine Learning API (WinML). These are so lightweight it doesn't really require 9000 GPUs and 120pb (Peanut Butters) of memory. It is written using Windows Runtime so it is accessible from C, C++, and anything in the .NET family. It will work natively across the Windows platform (although it is a pain in the ass in C).  If you're writing this in C/C++, the application flow goes as follows:  1. RoInitialize (although the original author did it all weird and ass backwards) 2. Get ONNX bytes (ReadFile, download, whatever) 3. Do dumb stream stuff 4. CreateFromStream (ONNX stream stuff) 5. LearningModelStatics->LoadFromStream  In the simplest terms possible, load the ONNX stuff into memory using the fancy-schmany Windows Runtime stuff and let the Windows ML API hang out with it.  In Hxr1's proof-of-concept the ONNX file (shrimple AI model) has a malicious payload baked inside of it. However, memory allocated from the dumb stream stuff, and the memory used by LearningModelStatics is NOT executable. To compensate for this, Hxr1 reads the content from the loaded ONNX file, copies it to a buffer allocated by VirtualAlloc, changes the allocated buffer to RWX, then does the malicious stuff.  This fact contradicts his entire paper. In essence, all the WinML API is doing in this context is a really roundabout way to do ReadFile. He also misses out on some other interesting opportunities.  1. Using the WinML is it (probably) possible to make an AI model that evaluates the machine to determine if it is a VM. @0xTriboulet did something similar where he used the WinML to hunt for credentials in documents on machines.  2. It may be possible to change the memory characteristics from LearningModelStatics to be RWX. However, I haven't tested this and this contains a massive asterisk because several conditions need to be met which I haven't evaluated.  hxr1.ghost.io/abusing-winml-…

### 1. 현재 주요 사이버보안 트렌드
최근 위협 환경은 AI 도구 및 오픈소스 라이브러리(Axios, Marimo)의 취약점을 악용한 공급망 공격과 제로데이 취약점(CVE-2026-34621)의 활발한 악용이 두드러집니다. 특히, 공격자들은 합법적인 소프트웨어 다운로드 사이트를 변조하거나 가짜 AI 서비스 웹사이트를 배포하여 PlugX, STX RAT 등 악성코드를 유포하는 전략을 구사하고 있습니다. 또한, 클라우드 환경에서의 IAM 권한 오남용 및 설정 오류를 노린 공격이 증가하고 있으며, 지정학적 갈등과 연계된 핵티비스트 그룹의 인프라 대상 파괴적 공격(Wiper)이 지속되고 있습니다.

### 2. 주요 위협 행위자 / 캠페인
*   **Handala**: UAE 정부 기관(Dubai Courts, Land Dept, Transport Authority)을 대상으로 대규모 데이터 유출 및 파괴적 공격 수행.
*   **STARDUST CHOLLIMA**: Axios npm 패키지 공급망 공격을 통해 악성코드 유포 시도.
*   **Kimsuky**: GitLab 플랫폼을 악용하여 이력서 및 대북 정책 문서로 위장한 악성 LNK 파일 유포.
*   **ShinyHunters**: Rockstar Games를 대상으로 데이터 유출 협박 및 금전 요구.

### 3. 한국 관련 위협
*   **Kimsuky**: 이스트시큐리티 알약 보고서에 따르면, GitLab을 활용하여 한국의 구직자 및 대북 관련 종사자를 타겟으로 한 악성 LNK 파일 유포 캠페인 포착.
*   **기타**: 한국어 트윗에서 직접적인 대규모 국내 피해 사례는 보고되지 않았으나, 글로벌 공급망 공격 및 Adobe 제로데이 취약점에 대한 주의가 요구됨.

### 4. 주요 CVE / 취약점
*   **CVE-2026-40175**: Axios, CVSS 10.0 (Critical), AWS IMDSv2 우회 및 RCE 가능, 공개 익스플로잇 존재.
*   **CVE-2026-34621**: Adobe Acrobat Reader, CVSS 8.6, 원격 코드 실행(RCE), 야생에서 활발히 악용 중.
*   **CVE-2026-35616**: Fortinet FortiClientEMS, 접근 제어 취약점, 야생에서 활발히 악용 중.
*   **CVE-2026-39987**: Marimo, Pre-auth RCE, 활발히 악용 중.
*   **CVE-2026-6168**: TOTOLINK A7000R, Elevated criticality, RCE/DoS 위험.

### 5. IOC 요약
*   **도메인**: cpuid[.]com ([TheHackerNews](https://thehackernews.com/2026/04/cpuid-breach-distributes-stx-rat-via.html))
*   **해시**: a8ba9ba93b4509a86e3d7dd40fd0652c ([SANS ISC](https://isc.sans.edu/diary/rss/32884))
*   **CVE**: CVE-2026-34621 ([TheHackerNews](https://thehackernews.com/2026/04/adobe-patches-actively-exploited.html)), CVE-2026-40175 ([Twitter](https://twitter.com/the_yellow_fall/status/2043376274047910003)), CVE-2026-35616 ([Twitter](https://twitter.com/dansantanna/status/2043452637048402141)), CVE-2026-39987 ([Twitter](https://twitter.com/JedisecX/status/2043440582832357494))

### 6. 즉각 대응 권고
*   **Adobe Acrobat Reader 긴급 패치**: CVE-2026-34621 대응을 위해 즉시 최신 버전으로 업데이트 수행.
*   **공급망 보안 점검**: 프로젝트 내 Axios 라이브러리 버전을 v1.15.0 이상으로 즉시 업데이트하고, npm 패키지 무결성 검증 강화.
*   **비인가 소프트웨어 차단**: CPUID 등 하드웨어 모니터링 툴 다운로드 시 공식 경로 확인 및 출처 불분명한 AI 서비스 사이트 접속 차단.
